mechanism: Small-caps are disproportionately sensitive to credit conditions due to higher leverage (3.0-3.5x vs 1.0-1.5x), higher floating-rate exposure (35-40% vs 15-20%), and lower interest coverage (~2.0x). The financial accelerator (Bernanke-Gertler-Gilchrist) transmits credit tightening more severely to smaller, more leveraged firms.
regime_dependence: Correlation with real fed funds rate of -0.65 to -0.75. Strongest during tightening cycles. The maturity wall concentration in 2025-2027 amplifies current sensitivity.
